Assembly: The assembly ilMusProo1.hap1.1 is based on 66x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was identified by synteny to GCA_905147235.1.

Assembly: The assembly nxAllPara2.1 is based on PacBio HiFi derived from a single male individual using the Picogram Input Multimodal Sequencing (PiMmS) method which was scaffolded using Hi-C data derived from a pool of 40 individuals. This genome was generated by the Single Nematode Genomes study within the Wellcome Sanger Institute's 959 Nematode Genomes project. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, contamination removal via blobtoolkit, and scaffolding with YaHS. Bipartite mitochondrial genomes were identified using Mitohifi (15.3 kb mitochondrial genome with 7 PCG including Cox2) and tblastn (14.6 kb mitochondrial genome with 6 unidirectional PCG including Cox1).

Assembly: The assembly inNotCapi1.hap1.1 is based on 33x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sex chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number and read coverage. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome Z (0-987kbp) and chromosome W (2.8-8.1 Mbp) is unknown. Additional sequence of the W chromosome might be in the small unlocalised fragments.

Assembly: The assembly drAilAlti1.1 is based on 45x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Several small, repetitive, scaffolds localise equally well to both SUPER_28 and SUPER_9. They have been placed with SUPER_9, though placement is uncertain.
